septal
  • API reference
  • Hessian
  • View page source

Hessian

Damped BFGS Hessian approximation (Powell, 1978).

The standard BFGS update is:

H_{k+1} = H_k - (H_k s_k s_kᵀ H_k) / (s_kᵀ H_k s_k)

  • (r_k r_kᵀ) / (s_kᵀ r_k)

where s_k = x_{k+1} - x_k and r_k is the (possibly damped) curvature vector.

Powell damping modifies y_k so that the updated Hessian remains positive definite, which is required for the QP subproblem to be convex:

θ_k = 1 if s_kᵀ y_k >= 0.2 · s_kᵀ H_k s_k

0.8 s_kᵀ H_k s_k otherwise ───────────────────── s_kᵀ H_k s_k - s_kᵀ y_k

r_k = θ_k y_k + (1 - θ_k) H_k s_k

Condition-number reset

After each BFGS update the spectral condition number of H is checked via jnp.linalg.eigvalsh. If cond(H) > max_cond the matrix is reset to mean(diag(H)) * I. This prevents the Hessian approximation from becoming ill-conditioned on problems with curved equality manifolds where the full-space Lagrangian Hessian is indefinite (e.g. hs006, hs039).

All operations are pure JAX — no Python control flow on traced values.

septal.jax.sqp.hessian.bfgs_update(H, s, y, skip_tol=1e-10, max_cond=100000000.0)[source]

Damped BFGS rank-2 Hessian update with condition-number safeguard.

Parameters:

  • H (Array) – Current Hessian approximation, shape (n, n). Must be symmetric positive definite.

  • s (Array) – Primal step x_{k+1} - x_k, shape (n,).

  • y (Array) – Lagrangian gradient difference ∇L_{k+1} - ∇L_k, shape (n,).

  • skip_tol (float) – If sᵀs < skip_tol the update is skipped (step was numerically zero).

  • max_cond (float) – If cond(H_new) > max_cond the Hessian is reset to mean(diag(H_new)) * I to prevent ill-conditioning.

Returns:

Updated Hessian, shape (n, n).

Return type:

Array

septal.jax.sqp.hessian.lagrangian_grad(x, lam, p, objective, constraints, n_g)[source]

Compute the Lagrangian gradient ∇_x L(x, λ, p).

Parameters:

  • x (Array) – Current iterate, shape (n,).

  • lam (Array) – Dual variables for general constraints, shape (n_g,).

  • p (Array) – Parameter vector, shape (m,).

  • objective (callable) – JAX callable f(x, p) -> scalar.

  • constraints (callable) – JAX callable g(x, p) -> (n_g,) or None.

  • n_g (int) – Number of general constraints.

Returns:

∇f(x, p) + J_g(x, p)ᵀ λ.

Return type:

Array

septal.jax.sqp.hessian.lagrangian_hessian(x, lam, p, objective, constraints, n_g)[source]

Compute the exact Lagrangian Hessian ∇²_xx L(x, λ, p) via jax.hessian.

Parameters:

  • x (Array) – Current iterate, shape (n,).

  • lam (Array) – Dual variables for general constraints, shape (n_g,).

  • p (Array) – Parameter vector, shape (m,).

  • objective (callable) – JAX callable f(x, p) -> scalar.

  • constraints (callable) – JAX callable g(x, p) -> (n_g,) or None.

  • n_g (int) – Number of general constraints.

Returns:

∇²f(x, p) + Σᵢ λᵢ ∇²gᵢ(x, p).

Return type:

Array

septal.jax.sqp.hessian.regularised_lagrangian_hessian(x, lam, p, objective, constraints, n_g, hess_reg_delta=0.0001, hess_reg_min=1e-08)[source]

Exact Lagrangian Hessian with adaptive PD regularisation.

Computes ∇²_xx L(x, λ, p) via jax.hessian then applies an additive shift tau * I to ensure all eigenvalues are at least hess_reg_delta. This guarantees the QP subproblem receives a positive-definite Hessian regardless of the iterate’s non-convexity.

Parameters:

  • x (Array) – See lagrangian_hessian().

  • lam (Array) – See lagrangian_hessian().

  • p (Array) – See lagrangian_hessian().

  • objective (callable) – See lagrangian_hessian().

  • constraints (callable) – See lagrangian_hessian().

  • n_g (int) – See lagrangian_hessian().

  • hess_reg_delta (float) – Target minimum eigenvalue. Shift is applied only when the smallest eigenvalue is below this threshold.

  • hess_reg_min (float) – Minimum shift always added (even when all eigenvalues exceed hess_reg_delta), preventing an exactly-zero shift that could leave borderline eigenvalues unprotected.

Returns:

Symmetrised, PD-regularised Lagrangian Hessian.

Return type:

Array

Choosing between BFGS and exact Hessian

BFGS (use_exact_hessian=False)

Exact (use_exact_hessian=True)

Cost per iter

\(O(n^2)\) (rank-2 update)

\(O(n^2)\) forward passes via jax.hessian

Accuracy

Approximate (improves over iterations)

Exact (full second-order information)

PD guarantee

By construction (damped BFGS)

Via inertia correction \(\tau I\)

Recommended for

Large \(n\), smooth objectives

Small \(n\), highly non-convex or constrained

Note

regularised_lagrangian_hessian applies an additive shift \(\tau I\) where \(\tau = \max(\delta_\text{min}, -\lambda_\text{min}(H) + \delta)\). This guarantees all eigenvalues \(\ge \delta\), keeping the QP Hessian PD even on non-convex constraint manifolds.
